step_by_step_thinking: Electrical conductivity speed of A alpha fibres ? Based on the articles reviewed, particularly article-25918_2 and others discussing nerve fiber classifications and properties, we understand that A-alpha fibers belong to the category of myelinated fibers. Article-25918_2 specifies that A-alpha fibers are part of the group designated for sensation involving light touch, vibration, and proprioception, corresponding to larger diameters and faster conduction velocities among myelinated fibers. Given that A-alpha falls under the broader class of myelinated fibers, it's expected that their conduction velocity would fall towards the upper limit of typical values reported for similar myelinated nerve fibers. Specifically, it mentions that myelinated motor and sensory nerve axons can reach conduction velocities up to 150 m/s. Therefore, considering the context and comparison with analogous myelinated structures like A-delta and given its functional attributes related to finer touch and deep-pressure sense, reasonable estimates place A-alpha fibers' conduction speeds appropriately high yet slightly below the absolute maximum mentioned here.

Electrical conductivity speed of A alpha fibres ? Based on the articles reviewed, particularly article-25918_2 and others discussing nerve fiber classifications and properties, we understand that A-alpha fibers belong to the category of myelinated fibers. Article-25918_2 specifies that A-alpha fibers are part of the group designated for sensation involving light touch, vibration, and proprioception, corresponding to larger diameters and faster conduction velocities among myelinated fibers. Given that A-alpha falls under the broader class of myelinated fibers, it's expected that their conduction velocity would fall towards the upper limit of typical values reported for similar myelinated nerve fibers. Specifically, it mentions that myelinated motor and sensory nerve axons can reach conduction velocities up to 150 m/s. Therefore, considering the context and comparison with analogous myelinated structures like A-delta and given its functional attributes related to finer touch and deep-pressure sense, reasonable estimates place A-alpha fibers' conduction speeds appropriately high yet slightly below the absolute maximum mentioned here.
\boxed{A}
